component.name: ResponsiveDialog
component.description: Adaptive dialog that renders a centered Dialog on desktop (≥ 768px) and either a vaul Drawer or a full-viewport Sheet on mobile, selected via the mobileVariant prop.

useCases: Edit forms, detail views, or confirmations that must work across all screen sizes
Any non-destructive dialog interaction in a responsive layout
Flows where mobile users benefit from a bottom sheet instead of a centered modal

commonPatterns.name: drawer-edit-form
commonPatterns.description: Default mobile experience — vaul drawer with natural height, drag-to-dismiss, glass top border
commonPatterns.composition:
<ResponsiveDialog>
  <ResponsiveDialogTrigger asChild><Button variant="outline">Edit details</Button></ResponsiveDialogTrigger>
  <ResponsiveDialogContent>
    <ResponsiveDialogHeader>
      <ResponsiveDialogTitle>Edit merchant details</ResponsiveDialogTitle>
      <ResponsiveDialogDescription>Changes take effect immediately.</ResponsiveDialogDescription>
    </ResponsiveDialogHeader>
    <Field><FieldLabel>Name</FieldLabel><FieldContent><Input /></FieldContent></Field>
    <ResponsiveDialogFooter>
      <ResponsiveDialogCancel>Cancel</ResponsiveDialogCancel>
      <ResponsiveDialogAction>Save changes</ResponsiveDialogAction>
    </ResponsiveDialogFooter>
  </ResponsiveDialogContent>
</ResponsiveDialog>

commonPatterns.name: sheet-full-viewport
commonPatterns.description: Full-screen mobile experience — Sheet covers the entire viewport
commonPatterns.composition:
New transaction Fill in the transaction details below.
...form content...
Cancel Submit

commonPatterns.name: single-action
commonPatterns.description: Informational dialog/drawer with one dismiss action and no cancel button
commonPatterns.composition:
Payment processed The payment was successfully captured.
...summary content...
Done

antiPatterns.scenario: Using ResponsiveDialog for destructive or irreversible actions
antiPatterns.reason: ResponsiveDialog is dismissible by dragging (drawer) or clicking outside (dialog); destructive actions must use ResponsiveAlertDialog, which locks the mobile Drawer and forces explicit acknowledgment
antiPatterns.alternative: Use ResponsiveAlertDialog for delete, remove, disconnect, or any irreversible action

antiPatterns.scenario: Using ResponsiveDialog for multi-step or lengthy workflows
antiPatterns.reason: Both Dialog and Drawer are designed for short, focused tasks. Multi-step flows create cramped modals and poor UX
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a dedicated full-page route or flow for multi-step processes

antiPatterns.scenario: Rendering only a ResponsiveDialogCancel with no ResponsiveDialogAction
antiPatterns.reason: A cancel-only footer serves no purpose; the X button and overlay/drag already dismiss the surface
antiPatterns.alternative: If there is only one action, render ResponsiveDialogAction alone. If no actions are needed, omit the footer entirely

size.note: Size applies to DialogContent on desktop only. Drawer and Sheet always use natural / full height.

semanticMeaning.drawer: Vaul drawer — glass top border, natural content height, drag-to-dismiss. Best for compact forms and detail views.
semanticMeaning.sheet: Full-viewport Sheet (side=bottom, h-svh, no rounding) — covers the entire screen. Best for longer forms or immersive mobile tasks.

slots.trigger: ResponsiveDialogTrigger — routes to DialogTrigger, DrawerTrigger, or SheetTrigger based on breakpoint and mobileVariant
slots.content: ResponsiveDialogContent — routes to DialogContent (desktop), DrawerContent (mobile drawer), or SheetContent side=bottom (mobile sheet)
slots.header: ResponsiveDialogHeader — routes to DialogHeader, DrawerHeader, or SheetHeader
slots.title: ResponsiveDialogTitle — routes to DialogTitle, DrawerTitle, or SheetTitle
slots.description: ResponsiveDialogDescription — routes to DialogDescription, DrawerDescription, or SheetDescription
slots.footer: ResponsiveDialogFooter — routes to DialogFooter, DrawerFooter, or SheetFooter
slots.action: ResponsiveDialogAction — primary button; closes the surface on click. Renders DialogAction on desktop, Button variant=primary + DrawerClose/SheetClose on mobile.
slots.cancel: ResponsiveDialogCancel — outline button; closes the surface without action. Only render alongside ResponsiveDialogAction.
slots.close: ResponsiveDialogClose — low-level dismiss primitive. Routes to DialogClose, DrawerClose, or SheetClose.

parentConstraints: All Responsive* sub-components must be descendants of ResponsiveDialog
ResponsiveDialogAction and ResponsiveDialogCancel must be inside ResponsiveDialogFooter
ResponsiveDialogTitle must be inside ResponsiveDialogContent for accessibility

interactions.desktop-open: Dialog overlay fades in with backdrop blur; content zooms in with fade
interactions.desktop-close: Content zooms out with fade; overlay fades out. Dismissible via X button, Escape, or clicking overlay.
interactions.mobile-drawer-open: Drawer slides up from bottom with glass top border; drag handle visible
interactions.mobile-drawer-close: Drag down or tap overlay to dismiss; action/cancel buttons also close
interactions.mobile-sheet-open: Sheet slides up to cover full viewport (h-svh, no rounding)
interactions.mobile-sheet-close: Dismissible via action/cancel buttons or Escape

responsive.mobile: < 768px: renders Drawer (default) or Sheet depending on mobileVariant
responsive.desktop: ≥ 768px: renders centered Dialog with sm (max-w-80) or default (max-w-lg) size

accessibility.role: dialog on desktop (Radix Dialog); dialog on mobile drawer (vaul); complementary on mobile sheet (Radix Sheet)
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Escape closes all surfaces; Tab cycles focus within content; Enter/Space activates buttons
accessibility.screenReader: Announced as a dialog on all surfaces; title and description associated via aria attributes; focus trapped within open surface
accessibility.focusManagement: Focus trapped inside the open surface; returns to trigger element on close

rules: Use ResponsiveDialog as a drop-in replacement for Dialog whenever the UI must work on mobile.
Default mobileVariant is 'drawer' — use 'sheet' only when content is long enough to warrant a full-screen takeover.
Never use ResponsiveDialog for destructive or irreversible actions — use ResponsiveAlertDialog instead.
ResponsiveDialogCancel must only appear alongside ResponsiveDialogAction. Never render it alone.
ResponsiveDialogAction defaults to size='sm'; ResponsiveDialogCancel defaults to variant='outline' size='sm'.
ResponsiveDialogTitle is always required inside ResponsiveDialogContent for screen reader accessibility.
The mobileVariant prop is set on the root ResponsiveDialog and flows down via context — do not set it on sub-components.

aiHints.context: Use ResponsiveDialog instead of Dialog whenever the interface must work on both desktop and mobile. It renders a standard Dialog on desktop and either a Drawer (default, natural height, drag-to-dismiss) or a full-screen Sheet on mobile. Do NOT use for destructive actions — use ResponsiveAlertDialog, which preserves the blocking safety contract on all screen sizes. The mobileVariant prop controls the mobile surface: 'drawer' for compact interactions, 'sheet' for longer forms.
